## Current State
- Repository: `/Users/donaldfilimon/abi`
- Active branch: `main`
- `main` is up to date with `origin/main` after pushing merge commit `6a66d5e0`.
- Local branches:
  - `integrate-all-work` -> `938c64ca`
  - `integrate-stash-work` -> `938c64ca`
  - `plan-next-zigmaster-steps` -> `86ec21c7`
  - `please-review-my-uncommitted-changes` -> `5517cf96`
  - `main` -> `6a66d5e0`
- Every non-`main` local branch is already an ancestor of `main`.
- Remote branches:
  - `origin/main`
  - `origin/please-review-my-uncommitted-changes`
- `origin/please-review-my-uncommitted-changes` is already merged via `origin/main`.
- There is one linked worktree at `/Users/donaldfilimon/.codex/worktrees/9a24/abi` attached to `please-review-my-uncommitted-changes`.

## Constraints / Non-Goals
- Do not rewrite history.
- Do not delete `main` or `origin/main`.
- Delete only refs that are confirmed merged into `main`.
- If the linked worktree has uncommitted changes, preserve them before removing the worktree or deleting its branch.

## Success Criteria
- Confirm whether any branch still needs merging into `main`.
- Recommend the safe order of operations for linked worktree handling, local branch deletion, and remote branch deletion.
- Call out any branch or worktree that should be preserved despite being merged.

## Decision Question
- Given this branch topology, what is the safest cleanup sequence to end with only useful branches remaining while avoiding loss of work in the linked worktree?
